contracts.term_scope_work: The Supplier undertakes to supply, deliver and (where applicable) install the goods and/or services described in ":title" strictly in accordance with the technical specifications, quantities, drawings and acceptance criteria forming an integral part of this Agreement.
contracts.term_scope_quality: All deliverables shall conform to the applicable Emirates Authority for Standardization and Metrology (ESMA / MoIAT) standards, the GCC Standardisation Organisation (GSO) standards and any sector-specific regulations in force in the United Arab Emirates as of the effective date.
contracts.term_total_value: The total consideration payable under this Agreement is :amount, of which the Value Added Tax (VAT) component (where applicable) has been calculated in accordance with Federal Decree-Law No. 8 of 2017 on Value Added Tax and Cabinet Decision No. 52 of 2017 issuing its Executive Regulations.
contracts.term_payment_breakdown: Payments shall be released against the following milestone schedule: :terms. Each milestone constitutes a separate, distinct supply for VAT invoicing purposes.
contracts.term_payment_method: Payments shall be effected by bank transfer to the Supplier's IBAN nominated in writing, within seven (7) business days of the Buyer's approval of the corresponding milestone deliverable. Late payment shall accrue compensatory interest at the legal rate, capped at twelve per cent (12%) per annum, in line with the cap recognised by UAE jurisprudence under Article 76 of Federal Decree-Law No. 50 of 2022 (Commercial Transactions Law).
contracts.term_delivery_days: Delivery shall be completed within :days calendar days from the Effective Date. Time is of the essence within the meaning of Article 247 of Federal Law No. 5 of 1985 (Civil Transactions Code).
contracts.term_delivery_inspection: The Buyer shall have five (5) business days from physical receipt to inspect the goods and notify the Supplier in writing of any non-conformity, defect or shortage. Failure to notify within this period shall be deemed acceptance, save for latent defects, which remain governed by Articles 543–548 of the Civil Transactions Code.
contracts.term_delivery_risk: Unless expressly stated otherwise in the Schedule, delivery shall be DAP (Incoterms® 2020) at the Buyer's nominated address inside the United Arab Emirates. Title and risk of loss pass to the Buyer upon successful delivery and acceptance at that location.
contracts.term_warranty_period: The Supplier warrants that the goods shall be free from defects in material, workmanship and design, and shall remain fit for their stated purpose for a period of twelve (12) months from the date of acceptance, without prejudice to the ten-year decennial liability set out in Article 880 of the Civil Transactions Code where structural works are involved.
contracts.term_warranty_scope: The Supplier shall, at its own cost, repair, replace or refund any defective deliverable within fourteen (14) business days of written notice. The warranty does not cover damage caused by misuse, unauthorised modification, or normal wear and tear.
contracts.term_confidentiality_1: Each party undertakes to keep all non-public technical, commercial and financial information disclosed under this Agreement strictly confidential and to use it solely for the purpose of performing this Agreement.
contracts.term_confidentiality_2: The confidentiality obligations shall survive termination of this Agreement for three (3) years and are without prejudice to the parties' obligations under Federal Decree-Law No. 45 of 2021 on the Protection of Personal Data (the "PDPL") and any implementing regulations issued by the UAE Data Office.
contracts.term_disputes_negotiation: The parties shall first attempt to resolve any dispute amicably through good-faith negotiation between authorised representatives within thirty (30) days of a written notice of dispute.
contracts.term_disputes_jurisdiction: Any dispute that cannot be resolved amicably shall be finally settled by arbitration administered by the Dubai International Arbitration Centre (DIAC) under its Arbitration Rules in force at the time of submission, before a sole arbitrator, seated in Dubai, United Arab Emirates, conducted in English with Arabic translation upon request. Pursuant to Decree No. 34 of 2021 issued by H.H. the Ruler of Dubai (which abolished the DIFC-LCIA Arbitration Centre and transferred all of its pending and future cases to DIAC), any reference in this Agreement or in prior correspondence to DIFC-LCIA shall be construed as a reference to DIAC. The award shall be final, binding and enforceable in any court of competent jurisdiction.
contracts.term_governing_law_1: This Agreement and any non-contractual obligations arising out of or in connection with it shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the federal laws of the United Arab Emirates, in particular Federal Law No. 5 of 1985 (Civil Transactions Code) and Federal Decree-Law No. 50 of 2022 (Commercial Transactions Law), together with the laws applicable in the Emirate where the Buyer is registered.
contracts.term_governing_law_2: Where this Agreement is performed within a financial free zone (DIFC or ADGM), the parties may, by written addendum, elect the law and courts of that free zone in lieu of onshore UAE law, in which case the elected regime shall prevail to the extent of any inconsistency.
contracts.term_vat_1: All amounts stated in this Agreement are exclusive of VAT unless expressly stated otherwise. The Supplier shall issue a valid Tax Invoice in accordance with Article 65 of Federal Decree-Law No. 8 of 2017 and Article 59 of Cabinet Decision No. 52 of 2017 within fourteen (14) days of each taxable supply.
contracts.term_vat_2: Where the Buyer is required to apply the reverse-charge mechanism on imported services, the Supplier shall issue a zero-rated invoice and the Buyer shall self-account for the output VAT in its tax return for the corresponding period.
contracts.term_esign_1: The parties agree that this Agreement and any amendments hereto may be executed by electronic signature, which shall be deemed a valid and binding signature equivalent to a handwritten signature in accordance with Federal Decree-Law No. 46 of 2021 on Electronic Transactions and Trust Services and its Executive Regulations.
contracts.term_esign_2: The platform shall record the signatory's identity, IP address, timestamp and a cryptographic hash of the executed document, and such audit trail shall be admissible as evidence before any UAE court or arbitral tribunal in accordance with Articles 17–18 of the said Decree-Law.
contracts.term_aml_1: Each party warrants that it shall comply with Federal Decree-Law No. 20 of 2018 on Anti-Money Laundering and Combating the Financing of Terrorism, Federal Decree-Law No. 31 of 2021 (Penal Code) provisions on bribery (Articles 234–236), and all UAE Cabinet decisions on targeted financial sanctions and the local terrorist list.
contracts.term_aml_2: Neither party, nor any of its directors, officers, employees, beneficial owners or affiliates, shall be a Sanctioned Person under any UAE, UN, US OFAC, EU or UK sanctions list. Breach of this clause entitles the non-defaulting party to terminate this Agreement immediately without liability.
contracts.term_pdpl_1: Each party shall process Personal Data exchanged under this Agreement solely for the purpose of performing it, and shall implement appropriate technical and organisational measures to protect such data in accordance with the PDPL (Federal Decree-Law No. 45 of 2021) and any sector-specific regulations issued by the UAE Data Office.
contracts.term_pdpl_2: Cross-border transfers of Personal Data outside the United Arab Emirates shall only be carried out to jurisdictions recognised by the UAE Data Office as providing an adequate level of protection, or pursuant to standard contractual clauses approved by the Office.
contracts.term_force_majeure_1: Neither party shall be liable for any failure or delay in performance caused by an event beyond its reasonable control (a "Force Majeure Event"), including acts of God, war, civil unrest, epidemic, government action, port closure, or import/export embargo, in line with Articles 273 and 287 of the Civil Transactions Code.
contracts.term_force_majeure_2: The affected party shall notify the other in writing within seven (7) days of becoming aware of the Force Majeure Event. If the event continues for more than sixty (60) consecutive days, either party may terminate this Agreement by written notice without further liability, save for amounts already due for performed deliverables.
contracts.term_termination_1: Either party may terminate this Agreement with immediate effect by written notice if the other party (a) commits a material breach which it fails to cure within fifteen (15) business days of written notice, (b) becomes insolvent, files for bankruptcy or enters into composition under Federal Decree-Law No. 51 of 2023 on Financial Restructuring and Bankruptcy, or (c) breaches the anti-bribery, sanctions or AML undertakings of this Agreement.
contracts.term_termination_2: On termination, the Supplier shall hand over all completed deliverables and the Buyer shall pay for all work satisfactorily performed up to the effective termination date, less any proven damages.
contracts.term_notices_1: All notices under this Agreement shall be in writing in Arabic or English and delivered through the TriLink platform, by registered post, by courier or by e-mail to the address recorded in each party's verified company profile. Notices delivered through the platform shall be deemed received on the date of the platform's delivery receipt.
contracts.term_assignment_1: Neither party may assign or transfer any right or obligation under this Agreement without the prior written consent of the other party, save that the Buyer may assign to an affiliate within the same group of companies upon prior written notice.
contracts.term_severability_1: If any provision of this Agreement is held invalid, unlawful or unenforceable by a court or tribunal of competent jurisdiction, the remaining provisions shall continue in full force and effect, and the parties shall negotiate in good faith a substitute provision reflecting their original commercial intent.
contracts.term_severability_2: This Agreement, together with all Schedules, the underlying RFQ, the accepted Bid and the Payment Schedule, constitutes the entire agreement between the parties and supersedes all prior representations, understandings and arrangements, whether oral or written, relating to its subject matter.
contracts.term_language_1: This Agreement is executed in bilingual Arabic / English form. Both versions are deemed authentic; in the event of any discrepancy of interpretation, the Arabic version shall prevail before UAE courts and government authorities, in line with Article 5 of Federal Law No. 26 of 1981 on the Arabic language.

demo.meta_title: Live Demo — TriLink Trading Platform Walkthrough
demo.hero_title:
See TriLink in action,
step by step
demo.hero_subtitle: A guided tour of every module on the TriLink platform — from raising a purchase request, all the way to final delivery, payment, and after-sales support. No sign-up required.
demo.cta_title: Ready to try it on real data?
demo.cta_subtitle: Create a free account and you can raise your first purchase request, invite suppliers, and walk through the full flow in under five minutes.
demo.section_01_body: TriLink is a regional B2B procurement and trade platform built for the GCC and the wider MENA market. It digitizes the complete trade lifecycle in one place — buyers, suppliers, logistics, customs, finance, sales, and government oversight all collaborate on the same tickets, contracts, and shipments.
demo.highlight_unified_d: No more email-and-spreadsheet handoffs between procurement, finance, and logistics.
demo.highlight_audit_d: Every approval, bid, payment, and shipment scan is recorded with timestamps and actors.
demo.highlight_bilingual_d: Switch between Arabic and English instantly. Forms, PDFs, and emails follow the user.
demo.highlight_compliance_d: KYC, beneficial ownership, insurance, ESG and customs paperwork captured up-front.
demo.section_02_title: Built for every stakeholder in trade
demo.section_02_subtitle: Each role gets a tailored dashboard, permissions, and workflow — no clutter, no irrelevant menus.
demo.role_company_manager_d: Approves purchase requests, invites team members, sets policies and spending limits.
demo.role_branch_manager_d: Runs the procurement of a single branch and rolls reporting up to HQ.
demo.role_buyer_d: Creates purchase requests, evaluates supplier bids, accepts or counter-offers.
demo.role_supplier_d: Receives RFQs, submits bids with payment schedules, signs contracts and ships goods.
demo.role_logistics_d: Books carriers, attaches AWB / BL documents, posts live shipment milestones.
demo.role_clearance_d: Files customs declarations, uploads HS codes, certificates of origin and duty receipts.
demo.role_finance_d: Releases milestone payments, reconciles invoices, manages escrow & refunds.
demo.role_sales_d: Manages the supplier-side product catalog, negotiations, and pipeline.
demo.role_service_provider_d: Offers value-added services (inspection, packaging, finance) attached to RFQs.
demo.role_government_d: Read-only oversight of registered companies, transactions, and compliance status.
demo.role_admin_d: Approves new companies, manages categories, settings, audit logs and disputes.
demo.role_finance_manager_d: Oversees the finance team, approves high-value disbursements, signs off on reports.
demo.section_03_subtitle: Follow a single purchase from the moment a buyer raises it, all the way to the final stamped delivery note.
demo.flow_01_body: A buyer fills in items, quantity, target budget, delivery date and required certifications. Drafts auto-save and the request lands in the manager's approval queue.
demo.flow_02_body: On approval, TriLink converts the PR into a public RFQ and notifies every supplier in the matching category. A countdown timer is shared with all participants.
demo.flow_03_body: Suppliers see the RFQ in their dashboard, attach pricing, lead time, technical compliance and a milestone payment schedule. Buyers can negotiate or ask for revisions.
demo.flow_04_body: The accepted bid mints a draft contract with both parties, line items and payment milestones pre-filled. Both sides e-sign in the browser; the PDF is hashed and stored.
demo.flow_05_body: When milestones complete, finance reviews supporting documents and releases each tranche. Optional escrow holds funds until the buyer confirms receipt.
demo.flow_06_body: Logistics partners book carriers and post live milestones. Clearance agents upload HS codes, certificates of origin and duty receipts so customs paperwork is never missed.
demo.flow_07_body: The buyer confirms delivery and rates the supplier. Disputes can be opened if anything is off-spec. Performance scores feed back into the supplier directory for next time.
demo.section_04_subtitle: Twenty-plus production-ready modules covering procurement, finance, logistics, compliance and analytics.
demo.module_pr_d: Drafts, approvals, multi-line items, attachments and budget validation.
demo.module_rfq_d: Public RFQs, sealed-bid auctions, reverse auctions with countdowns.
demo.module_bids_d: Side-by-side bid compare, Q&A, counter-offers, multi-round negotiations.
demo.module_contracts_d: Generated from accepted bids, multi-party signing, PDF + hash.
demo.module_milestones_d: JSON payment schedules, partial releases, FX-ready and reconciliation.
demo.module_escrow_d: Optional escrow per contract — funds released only when buyer confirms.
demo.module_spend_d: Category, supplier and branch breakdowns, savings vs. budget.
demo.module_shipment_d: Live milestones, AWB / BL upload, multi-leg routes, ETAs.
demo.module_quotes_d: Carriers compete on price and lead time, attached to a shipment.
demo.module_customs_d: HS codes, certificates of origin, duty receipts and broker hand-off.
demo.module_categories_d: Hierarchical industry categories used to match RFQs to suppliers.
demo.module_products_d: Supplier-side catalog with photos, pricing tiers and stock.
demo.module_directory_d: Public, SEO-friendly supplier profiles with rating and history.
demo.module_kyc_d: Trade license, VAT cert, beneficial owners — admin verification queue.
demo.module_insurance_d: Per-company policies and per-shipment cargo insurance attachments.
demo.module_esg_d: Carbon footprint, sustainability scoring and reporting per supplier.
demo.module_disputes_d: Open, evidence upload, admin arbitration and refund actions.
demo.module_performance_d: On-time delivery, quality, response time and overall supplier score.
demo.module_notifications_d: In-app + email digests with deep links to the relevant entity.
demo.module_audit_d: Every action recorded with actor, IP, before/after — searchable.
demo.module_branches_d: Multi-branch companies, role-aware invitations and permissions.
demo.module_api_d: Personal API tokens, webhooks, ERP/CRM hooks for external systems.
demo.module_ai_d: Natural-language search across PRs, contracts, suppliers and reports.
demo.module_loc_d: Bilingual AR/EN with full RTL — UI, PDFs and notifications.
demo.section_05_subtitle: Production-grade security, full audit trail, and compliance hooks for regulated industries.
demo.trust_rbac_d: 13 roles with granular per-action permissions, plus per-user allow-listing.
demo.trust_audit_d: Every CRUD action stamped with actor, IP, user-agent, before/after JSON.
demo.trust_2fa_d: TOTP-based 2FA with backup codes and per-device session control.
demo.trust_files_d: Private documents on isolated disks; only signed URLs for downloads.
demo.trust_kyc_d: Trade license, VAT cert and UBO declarations gated behind admin approval.
demo.trust_government_d: Read-only government accounts can audit any company's activity in real time.

privacy.body_who_we_are: TriLink is a B2B procurement platform operated in the United Arab Emirates. We are the data controller for the personal data described in this policy.
privacy.body_what_we_collect: We collect only the personal data we need to operate the procurement workflows you ask us to run on your behalf:
privacy.collect_account: Account details (name, email, phone, role within your company)
privacy.collect_company: Company information (trade licence, tax registration number, address, banking details)
privacy.collect_kyc: KYC and beneficial ownership data required by UAE AML laws (Federal Decree-Law 20/2018)
privacy.collect_transactional: Transactional data — purchase requests, RFQs, bids, contracts, payments, shipments
privacy.collect_technical: Technical data — IP address, browser, audit log entries (mandatory for security and compliance)
privacy.body_legal_basis: We process your data on the basis of: (a) performance of our contract with you and your company, (b) compliance with UAE legal obligations including AML, VAT and commercial transactions law, (c) your explicit consent for marketing communications and optional analytics cookies, and (d) our legitimate interest in keeping the platform secure.
privacy.body_residency: Your personal data is hosted in the :region region under the legal basis: :basis. Any sub-processor that handles your data is listed below; transfers outside the UAE rely on either an adequacy decision or signed Standard Contractual Clauses.
privacy.body_your_rights: Federal Decree-Law 45 of 2021 grants you the following rights, exercisable at any time from the Privacy & Data screen in your dashboard:
privacy.right_access: Access — receive a copy of every piece of personal data we hold about you
privacy.right_rectify: Rectification — correct any inaccurate or outdated personal data
privacy.right_erase: Erasure — request that we delete your personal data subject to lawful retention obligations
privacy.right_object: Objection — object to processing based on legitimate interest
privacy.right_withdraw: Withdraw consent — for marketing or optional cookies, at any time, without affecting prior lawful processing
privacy.section_retention: How long we keep your data
privacy.body_retention: Commercial and tax records are kept for at least five years from the end of the relationship as required by Federal Decree-Law 50/2022 and Federal Decree-Law 8/2017. Audit logs are kept for seven years. Marketing consent records are kept for as long as the consent is active and for two years after withdrawal as proof.
privacy.body_security: Sensitive personal data — Emirates ID, passport number, beneficial owner financial profile, IBAN — is encrypted at rest with AES-256-CBC. All connections use TLS. Access is restricted by role-based permissions and every access is logged in a tamper-evident audit trail.
privacy.body_breach: If a personal data breach is likely to harm your rights and freedoms, we will notify you and the UAE Data Office within 72 hours of becoming aware of the breach, in accordance with PDPL Article 9.
privacy.body_complaints: If you believe we have processed your personal data unlawfully you may file a complaint directly with the UAE Data Office. We would also welcome hearing from you first so we can try to resolve the matter directly.
privacy.dpa_purpose_body: This Data Processing Agreement governs the processing of personal data by TriLink (the Processor) on behalf of your company (the Controller) in connection with your use of the platform.
privacy.dpa_def_controller: Your company, which determines the purposes and means of processing of personal data submitted to the platform.
privacy.dpa_def_processor: TriLink, which processes personal data only on documented instructions from the Controller.
privacy.dpa_def_subject: Any natural person whose personal data is processed — your employees, beneficial owners, suppliers' representatives.
privacy.dpa_obligation_1: Process personal data only on documented instructions from the Controller, including transfers outside the UAE.
privacy.dpa_obligation_2: Ensure all personnel authorised to access personal data are bound by confidentiality obligations.
privacy.dpa_obligation_3: Implement appropriate technical and organisational measures, including encryption at rest for sensitive fields.
privacy.dpa_obligation_4: Assist the Controller in responding to data subject requests and notify of personal data breaches without undue delay.
privacy.dpa_obligation_5: On termination, return or delete all personal data unless retention is required by law.
privacy.dpa_schedule_intro: The Controller authorises TriLink to engage the following sub-processors:
privacy.dpa_governing_body: This DPA is governed by Federal Decree-Law 45 of 2021 and the laws of the United Arab Emirates. Disputes are subject to the jurisdiction of UAE courts.

register.field_free_zone_help: We use this to apply the correct legal jurisdiction (federal, DIFC, or ADGM) and the correct VAT classification on every contract.
bids.fz_hint_designated_internal: Both parties are in VAT Designated Zones. Goods supplied between two Designated Zones are outside the scope of UAE VAT (Cabinet Decision 59/2017). The contract VAT clause will reflect this.
bids.fz_hint_reverse_charge: One party is in a Designated Zone and the other is on the mainland. The reverse-charge mechanism may apply — the recipient will self-account for VAT on their next return.
bids.fz_hint_jurisdiction_difc: Both parties are registered in DIFC. The contract will be governed by DIFC Contract Law and disputes will be heard by DIFC Courts.
bids.fz_hint_jurisdiction_adgm: Both parties are registered in ADGM. The contract will be governed by ADGM Application Regulations (English common law) and disputes will be heard by ADGM Courts.
bids.fz_hint_jurisdiction_mismatch: The buyer and supplier are in different jurisdictions (federal vs DIFC/ADGM). The contract will default to UAE federal law because mixed contracts are not enforceable in DIFC or ADGM Courts without an express opt-in.
contracts.term_vat_designated_zone_1: Both parties are registered in VAT Designated Zones (Cabinet Decision No. 59 of 2017 and amendments). Supplies of goods between two Designated Zones are treated as outside the scope of UAE VAT and shall be invoiced without UAE VAT.
contracts.term_vat_designated_zone_2: Services supplied between Designated Zones remain subject to standard 5% VAT unless an explicit zero-rating provision applies under the Federal Decree-Law No. 8 of 2017.
contracts.term_vat_reverse_charge_1: Where one party is in a VAT Designated Zone and the other is on the UAE mainland, the supply shall be subject to the reverse-charge mechanism set out in Article 48 of Federal Decree-Law No. 8 of 2017. The recipient shall self-account for the applicable VAT on its next tax return.
contracts.term_vat_reverse_charge_2: Both parties shall keep all records evidencing the reverse-charge treatment for the statutory five-year period required by Article 78 of Federal Decree-Law No. 8 of 2017.
contracts.term_governing_law_difc_1: This Agreement shall be governed by, and construed in accordance with, the laws of the Dubai International Financial Centre (DIFC), including without limitation the DIFC Contract Law (DIFC Law No. 6 of 2004) and the DIFC Companies Law (DIFC Law No. 5 of 2018).
contracts.term_governing_law_difc_2: Where DIFC laws are silent on a matter, the parties agree that English common-law principles as they apply within the DIFC shall fill any gaps, in accordance with DIFC Law No. 3 of 2004.
contracts.term_governing_law_adgm_1: This Agreement shall be governed by, and construed in accordance with, the laws of the Abu Dhabi Global Market (ADGM), including the ADGM Application Regulations 2015 which directly apply English common law and equity to ADGM contracts.
contracts.term_governing_law_adgm_2: Where ADGM Regulations are silent, the parties agree that the principles of English common law and equity in force in England and Wales shall apply.
contracts.term_disputes_difc_courts: Any dispute arising out of or in connection with this Agreement shall be subject to the exclusive jurisdiction of the Courts of the Dubai International Financial Centre (DIFC Courts), which the parties expressly agree are competent under DIFC Law No. 10 of 2004.
contracts.term_disputes_adgm_courts: Any dispute arising out of or in connection with this Agreement shall be subject to the exclusive jurisdiction of the Courts of the Abu Dhabi Global Market (ADGM Courts), constituted under ADGM Founding Law No. 4 of 2013.
